verses.text: 19:4 Jonathan addressed his father as the king out of reverence.
19:4 Jonathan addressed his father as the king out of reverence.
19:6 vowed: Violating a vow is a serious offense against God (see Num 30:2; Deut 23:21-23; cp. Eccl 5:4-5). But Saul soon disregarded his promise and again sought to kill David (1 Sam 19:9-24).
19:6 vowed: Violating a vow is a serious offense against God (see Num 30:2; Deut 23:21-23; cp. Eccl 5:4-5). But Saul soon disregarded his promise and again sought to kill David (1 Sam 19:9-24).

19:10 hurled his spear: See also 18:11; 20:33.
19:10 hurled his spear: See also 18:11; 20:33.
19:10 hurled his spear: See also 18:11; 20:33.

19:13 an idol: Hebrew teraphim, which were household idols (see Gen 31:30; Judg 17:5; 2 Kgs 23:24). The presence of an idol in the home of David and Michal might show the pervasive influence of pagan idol worship among the Israelites; perhaps the idol belonged to Michal. Apparently David did not object to its presence. (Cp. Rachel’s theft of her father’s household idols in Gen 31:19.) • Siblings Jonathan (1 Sam 19:1-7) and Michal (19:11-18) saved David from their father and undermined their father’s plans to eliminate him. Members of Saul’s royal family played a significant role in David’s rise, though David did not usurp Saul’s throne.

19:18 Ramah: Samuel’s hometown (1:1). • Naioth was a tiny village not far from the other Ramah in Benjamin.
19:18 Ramah: Samuel’s hometown (1:1). • Naioth was a tiny village not far from the other Ramah in Benjamin.
19:20 Prophets often assembled in groups under a single leader (see 2 Kgs 2:15; 4:38; 6:1-7). • The Spirit of God came upon Saul’s men to allow David to escape (1 Sam 20:1).

otros mensajeros ... por tercera vez: Cada vez que Saúl enviaba tropas, los posibles captores de David eran capturados por el Espíritu. Profetizar los desviaba de perseguir a David.


19:24	d739023					Cuando Saúl se rasgó la ropa y se quedó desnudo, sin darse cuenta, actuó su verdadera condición espiritual de haber sido despojado del reinado ( **13:14** , **15:10-11** , **23** ; ver Nota Temática sobre “ Actos de Señales Proféticas ”). • ¿También Saúl entre los profetas?": En **1 Sam 10:9-13** , Saúl estaba ascendiendo como el rey elegido por Dios; aquí, está descendiendo bajo el peso del rechazo de Dios.

19:24 When Saul tore off his clothes and lay naked, he unwittingly acted out his true spiritual condition of having been stripped of the kingship (13:14; 15:10-11, 23; see “Prophetic Sign Acts” Theme Note). • Is even Saul a prophet? In 1 Sam 10:9-13, Saul was rising as God’s chosen king; here, he is descending under the weight of God’s rejection.
